Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: 3
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

SV DISEASE: +25
Effect: Light Healing (Any Slot, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 2.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

stein

It works fine, just completed it with agnostic dark elf at lvl 8. alliance works too, went from aprehensive to indifferent to hand in book. why? old times sake. many items much better in the bazaar.
▲ Page top
nerfed or what
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2009 at 8:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592347333397
Scholar
244 posts
Be warned if you should feel like doing this quest
I think it has been nerfed.

I have an agnostic enchanter and I cannot get the Ogre's
to let me do the turn ins. I've slaughtered many Dwarf guards
and still nothing. I cannot even do the newbie Lizardman Meat
quest to raise faction.
Clurg and the Warrior guildmaster are indifferent to me, and
its not good enough.

I've finally raised my faction enough to do the Lizardman Meat
quest and after tons of tons of turn ins Clurg still does not
like me well enough and the alliance line of spells do not work
on him anymore. As much as I would like to do this quest for
old times sake it just doesn't seem I am going to be able to.
I've tried Ogre illusion and DE illusion both to no avail.

Edited, Dec 26th 2009 5:18am by heavydrop

7 posts
Hi All. Just trying this quest so my chanter could bake some more and as of today Frostbite is still broken. After reading many posts on other pages and such, I see that you must now cast identify on the Regurgitonic to see if it's bad or not. Guess I'll try that and go get a new one. That's a long run if it is so I would try it before you run all the way back to Frostbite in West Karana. Hope this helps everyone.

QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2004 at 8:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591793697396
View User Forum
383 posts
Lucy lists two steins (1HBlunt and normal). My guess (and I haven't read all the posts below - too many of them) is that the 1H Blunt version was an old reward for the quest and now the quest gives the non weapon version. This assumption is partly based on item numbers (6359 vs. 13380) and that I've not seen the 1HB version around.

Scholar
366 posts
I've been away from EQ entirely for two years, and I've got some knowledge gaps. Maybe you can help me with this one:

My Stein of Moggok matches the description above; it's primarily an INT item for me, not a weapon. But Magelo shows it as a 1HB weapon (6/28, which isn't great but not too bad). Are the new steins weapons? Was my stein a weapon two years ago and I don't remember it? And now it's not? What's up here?

▲ Page top
I hope this is not nerfed.
QuoteReply
# Oct 13 2003 at 7:01 PM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
I assume lvl 8 is because A Darkelf Enchanter can do this at 8. Id like to still think he/she would be agnostic. And still need to use "Alliance"

I hope this guy about 5 posts down is wrong, I hope they have not nerfed this. I now have a lvl 12 Highelf Enchanter, Agnostic. I have made several alt enchanters in my time, and stripped and remade them. With another Highelf Enchanter I did this whole quest, DarkElf form and all, but I forgot to cast alliance on the FINAL guy!!! I assume that would have raised him up one standing from indiferent or whatever to Aimiable. He basically thanked me for the book, and explained I should leave before I would be crushed.

I'd therefore assume an agnostic Darkelf would also have to cast alliance. If she chose whatever god Ogres are, she would not, but would suffer a lot as a chanty in her days. (if they can even pick that god)

So I am assuming the lvl and faction is all based on this one spell. I really do not want to wait untill 20 to get this (being Ogre Illusion)

So I hope they just did what I did, and frgot to cast and are mistaken.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.

152 posts
just cast alliance on the last guy anyway, eventually you will get to where you can run the whole quest with NO spells except gate, grabbing sows or buying potions helps but isnt necessary, im doing it in 20-30 mins depending on fish drops (i gave up and used 2nd char to kill koalindls), im actually warmly to clurg. ran it as a chanter worshiping quellious, and even the bashers are dubious. did it about 15 times though.

# May 29 2003 at 2:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362394062
58 posts
I think this is right, lemmee know otherwise

Because of a new patch, enchanter's illusion line of spells have been nerfed quite a bit. They can no longer change into a Dark Elf and have faction modifiers for anyone besides Dark Elves. To do this quest the easy way for an enchanter of the light you MUST have the level 20 Ogre illusion spell or the Ogres WILL kill you! I hate Verant for nerfing them so badly...*sigh*

Not warrior, make yourself an ubar Shadowknight.... why? first off, faction. Second off, harm touch. So you dont have to level the stupid mule. I suppose you could make anything human that can start in Quenos, I chose SK for those reasons. now one HT and bam, fish parts.

THE DOG SPOKE!? AAAAHHHHH!!!

But seriously, this quest has been messed up a LOT by bugs. I waited until 31 for my enchanter to get it because 1) I am cheap and wanted to quest it myself, and 2) I waited until they apparently fixed it for good before I wasted the compendium.

The only thing was I got past the Frostbite part but forgot to take off invis when I did the hand-in in Ogguk. The lovely bartender ate my compendium and didn't even ask for seconds. /sigh, those Ogres and their table manners.

RE: Nerf?
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2003 at 3:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760148599
169 posts
Last time I did this it was mid May I believe. For a couple of years that this quest exists Frostbite was bugged-fixed-bugged again half of the time. Make sure Ur faction is OK with Barbies & redo regurgitonic part. Good luck.

Yeah, I remember back when there was only eq classic chanties were always paying me to whack out koa fish so they didn't lose faction just so they didn't have to worry bout that dog bug. Dunno why they didn't just make an sk, but 25pp sounded pretty good to a lvl9 magi back then! ACtually...sounds good to a 52 magi now....mmm...money...
▲ Page top
Important Safety Tip!
QuoteReply
# Apr 07 2003 at 11:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786470999
25 posts
(Note: This is a dupe post from the Quest page...just in case someone reads one but not the other)

Okay, not so much a safety tip as a "Damn, now I have to do that all again" prevention tip.

I made level 12 tonight and decided to run the quest.

Made a trip to OOT and bought a dozen or so Kiola Nuts (Yes, I plan on doing this one several times)
They were just over 1 gold each, and there was no price difference between Indifferent and Amiable faction. Illusion: Wood Elf

The Eruds Tonic was easiest of all of 'em. No negative faction hits, and a positive for EruditeCitizens or some such.
less than a gold for the Kiss of Death. No Illusion, as I am an Erudite.

HoneyComb was probably the most annoying, since I couldn't find a bixie with one to save my life. I was using my ranger to track 'em. Finally managed to get enough, though. Also found what may be a bixie spawn point in E Commons and got lucky buying 6 off the vendor nearby. About a gold each.
Illusion: Halfling and an Alliance (which probably wasn't needed)

The Fish: Chose the No Negative Faction method. Lotta running, but what the hell.
A time saver for other who will be doing this repeatedly- After you get teh Regurgitonic and head back for W Karana, swing by Surefall on your way and get a new Nitrate. One less run next time.
No cost. Illusion: Gnome for Regurgitonic and Illusion: Human with an Alliance for the Nitrates and Frostbite.

Handed them all in to the SeaFarer's Roost (Illusion: Human + Alliance. Probably necessary, but didn't feel like testing to find out)

Headed off to Ogguk. Illusion: Dark Elf works fine to get in. The Bartender is way the hell in the back of the zone. Don't forget to reapply Illuson if you aren't really a Dark Elf
Here's that Tip I mentioned....
Alliance only works on one NPC at a time (I tested after the fact)
First time out, I allianced him, then the bouncer...and didn't recheck him. He ate my book.

Second time, I just Allianced him...no problem, and my faction at turn in was Indifferent.

I think I can probably do the whole thing in under 30 minutes at this point...assuming I have the Honeycomb and Kiola Nut to start out with.

Total cost (even buying the honeycomb from Vendors): around 4 plat.

Have fun, enjoy all the healthy running!

▲ Page top

# Apr 07 2003 at 6:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751199580
96 posts
No, just means someone used the charges.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 17 2003 at 9:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) lol by 35 you should know what charges are. A charge is when you use the effect. This has 3 charges. Someone used the effect 3 times, meaning it has no charges.
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 18 2003 at 2:22 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Can a high elf mage do this quest pretty well or would he have a difficult time doing it?
RE: who can do it???
QuoteReply
# Mar 18 2003 at 2:45 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245745802
View User Forum
Sage
950 posts
Quote:
Can a high elf mage do this quest pretty well or would he have a difficult time doing it?


As far as I know, the only class that can pull this quest off is an Enchanter. Even with the new illusion potions, you'd still have to have good enough faction with the Orges to get them to work with you. Enchanters have spells to raise their faction, you don't.
▲ Page top
RE: who can do it???
QuoteReply
# Aug 04 2003 at 11:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786133267
59 posts
I do tyhis witth my gnome necro with no problem

Frostbite broken
QuoteReply
# Mar 16 2003 at 12:01 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591785183102
7 posts
On Nameless, Frostbite is now handing the Regurgitonic back. I've reported this as a bug.
▲ Page top
RE: Frostbite broken
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2003 at 8:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591784549153
3 posts
It's not a bug. Occasionally you'll be given bad Regurgitonic by the zookeeper in Ak'Anon. You can have the 'Identify' spell cast on it to confirm that. Just destroy that one, get another, and try again.
▲ Page top
RE: Frostbite broken
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2003 at 11:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591753697717
13 posts
A quicker way to get a koalindl fish is to make a human warrior and start them in Qeynos. Camp your main guy by the pond and load your warrior. Even at lev. 1 the warrior can kill the fish, once you see a corpse, load your main guy and loot the koalindl fish corpse.
▲ Page top
Inky or Ogre?
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2003 at 1:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763352374
View User Forum
Scholar
49 posts
This may be a dumb question...I keep seeing people talk about enchanters using the DE illusion for Clurg. Is there a problem using the Ogre illusion (if you are already high enough level to ast it)?
▲ Page top
RE: Inky or Ogre?
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2003 at 6:17 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245745802
View User Forum
Sage
950 posts
Not at all, in fact Ogre is better for that part of the quest, it's just DE is a lvl 12 spell, and people who aren't yet lvl 20 use DE to do it.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2003 at 4:07 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786117729
4 posts
I play a high elf wizard on the test server (so everything bad that can happen, happens here first). I started following the directions (found on http://www.graffe.com) and guess what, half of the items are no drop. So I gathered the rest of the stuff, turned it in for the compendium, and it's no drop. How on earth is a wizard supposed to get one of the better items available for +int if they've nerfed the ability to pass anything onto an enchanter! While I'm not KOS to most of the Ogres, I am to the bouncers. I tried turning it in once already and ended up losing the compendium (you are an enemy to us, leave while you still can), and then getting my rear handed to me by one of the bouncers. I couldn't even gate fast enough. This is crazy. Any ideas anyone?
▲ Page top
RE: A Major Pain - now nerfed
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2003 at 6:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1704672973637
Guru
5,311 posts
You get it by buying it from an enchanter. Unless you want to spend countless hours working on faction with many different races and cities.

They used to sell for around 200pp (on Terris Thule) in the pre-bazaar days. Now they're slightly more expensive.

The reason enchanters can do it and the rest of us can't is that they have not only racial illusion spells, but also the "alliance" spell which increases their faction with individual NPCs.

If you really don't want to spend the money for it, create an enchanter, level up to 8 or 12, depending on the race you choose, and do the quest yourself. That's exactly what I did when I was lower level. My little DE enchantress supported 2 accounts worth of toons doing this quest numerous times.
▲ Page top
RE: A Major Pain - now nerfed
QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2003 at 3:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785916408
View User Forum
10 posts
Can someone help me too? I am a level 9 Erudite Enchantress. I went to Kimble Nothrop (spelling?) and tried to get him to turn the nitrates into regurgatonic and he was a bit rude LOL So I cast Alliance and he gave it to me. Cast Alliance on Frostbite and he coughed up the fish. Cast Alliance in Rivervale and got the Honey Jum. Turned the ingrediants into the guy at the Seafarers and got the compendium. Put the compendium in the bank. Guess I got a bit greedy because I did it all again, tried to give the ingrediants to the guy and he gave me 2PP and no book.

Figured I'd give the book to Clurg. Invisible all the way through Ogguk, got into the bar, clicked on Clurg to cast Alliance, invisible went away - BAM! Guard knocked me down flat with one blow! Tried it again by using Illusion:Half Elf - BAM! on my butt again. Why does this site say that a level 8(9) Enchanter can do the quest when you don't even get to USE the Illusion:Dark Elf spell until level 12? And I haven't seen anyone mention using Illusion:Ogre, can this be done? What can I do as a level 9 Enchantress to get my stein?
▲ Page top
RE: A Major Pain - now nerfed
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2003 at 6:14 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245745802
View User Forum
Sage
950 posts
Illusion: Ogre is a lvl 20 spell. Even at lvl 12, becoming a Dark Elf and casting Alliance is not a gaurantee that you will get the stein for the book, depending on your natural faction. However at lvl 20 you also get a better faction spell, so any enchanter can do this once they hit lvl 20.
▲ Page top
RE: A Major Pain - now nerfed
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2003 at 3:20 PM Rating: Decent
MovarlThunderhawk
4 posts
A lvl 8 Dark Elf Enchanter can do this quest cause that is when we get Alliance, but the rest of us Enchanters have to wait till level 12 cause that is when we get Illusion Dark Elf.

62 Level High Elf Enchanter

RE: Magician
QuoteReply
# Apr 07 2003 at 6:11 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751199580
96 posts
? Last I checked, my druid could still talk to all the Ogres in Feerott (and presumably Ogguk, since those are mostly the same factions) while in wolf form.
Wolf form only effects faction in Old-world zones IIRC, and has no effect (or a negative effect) on faction in any of the expansion zones. I don't know where you got your info about it being nerfed post-PoP, but it was not.

What I want to know is why my Bear Form spell doesn't fool ANYONE...not bears, not NPCs, not anyone, not anywhere.

I mean, it's like a bad joke..."A bear and a wolf walk into a bar, and the bartender says 'HEY, Shaman, keep your wolf pet outside', and the wolf says 'I'm not a wolf pet, I'm a Druid!'"

Ok, I said it was a bad joke...

RE: Magician
QuoteReply
# Nov 25 2002 at 7:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773994568
Guru
658 posts
The part in Oggok can be done in Wolf form. You can either be a Druid (obviously) or recruit a friendly Druid to Wolf Form you inside Oggok itself. After you do this quest several times you'll be non-KOS in Oggok in your normal form. Even a Tunare-worshipping Wood Elf druid can walk the streets of Oggok safely (in elf form) after doing this quest a half-dozen times or so.
▲ Page top
Is it broke
QuoteReply
# Oct 22 2002 at 1:46 PM Rating: Decent
MovarlThunderhawk
4 posts
I have been doing the stein quest a lot recently and last week I tried to turn the regurgitonic into frostbite, but he said "I do not need that item" and gave it back to me. I tried again today with the PoP expansion hoping it was a bug and was fixed but no luck. Anyone have any ideas. BTW this is on the Tribunal server.

Update:
My faction was a little screwed, but I fixed it to where I am now indifferent. Still no luck with Frostbite. Going to try to get my faction to kindly and see what happens.

Edited, Sat Nov 16 23:18:24 2002
▲ Page top
Item is broke, not the quest
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2002 at 5:32 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592255411185
3 posts
I had a similar problem. No matter how many times I tried I just could not get Frostbite to take the regugatonic. i destroyed it, got the flask again, went to Ak'anon to get the regurgatonic, and when I came back, Frostbite took the regurgatonic. I've tried on and off for the last few patches, so I suspect it is the item itself that is bugged somehow.

I suggest getting the regurgatonic again, and try with a whole new item.

# Oct 24 2002 at 11:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245745802

Sage
950 posts
It's a bug that has been reported, seems to come and go. See the "Wolf Nerfed?" thread posted in May.

I haven't heard of it having anything to do with Barbarian faction before....have you done anything to FUBAR your Barb faction? I assume you're an Enchanter, and can therefore remedy any pesky faction issues with a spell.

If not, it's a bug and should be reported as such.
